intro: Solomon's Porch was a colonnade running along the eastern side of the temple mount, so called because it was traditionally regarded as a remnant of Solomon's original temple complex (Josephus, Antiq. 20.9.7). Whether this claim is accurate is uncertain, but the colonnade's name persisted into the New Testament period. Jesus walked in Solomon's Porch during the Feast of Dedication when the Jews pressed him about his identity (John 10:22–23). After the healing of the lame man, Peter and John were there with the astonished crowd (Acts 3:11), and the early Jerusalem church regularly gathered there (Acts 5:12).
